page.content: In the Connected vehicle service revenue table for Stellantis covering the years 2020, 2021, and 2022, the data reveals significant trends in automotive and mobility metrics. In 2020, the units sold reached 500,000, generating service revenue of $200M, with 1,000,000 connected vehicles and 100,000 active subscriptions, resulting in a customer satisfaction rate of 85% and a market share of 15%. The following year, 2021, saw an increase in units sold to 600,000, which corresponded to a rise in service revenue to $250M, while the number of connected vehicles grew to 1,200,000, and active subscriptions increased to 150,000, leading to an improvement in customer satisfaction to 90% and an enhanced market share of 18%. In 2022, units sold further increased to 700,000, with service revenue escalating to $300M. The total number of connected vehicles reached 1,500,000, and active subscriptions rose to 200,000, alongside a customer satisfaction rate of 92% and a market share of 20%. Cumulatively, across the three years, Stellantis achieved total units sold of 1,800,000, with consolidated service revenue amounting to $750M, resulting in a total of 3,700,000 connected vehicles and 450,000 active subscriptions, with an overall customer satisfaction average of 89% and a market share of 20%. The growth rates during this period demonstrate an annual increase of 14% in units sold, 25% in service revenue, 20% in connected vehicles, and 20% in active subscriptions, culminating in an annual revenue total of $750M.
